mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month upward trend in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which pushes corporate earnings higher and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO momentum (3-month change) against 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal has a negative directional consistency less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, the labor market becomes excessively tight, triggering wage inflation fears that force the Fed to tighten policy, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4%, the median 20-day forward return is positive, or the signal generates a hit ratio below 40% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, making equities more attractive relative to bonds and boosting stock prices over the following month as investors price in accommodative real conditions.
regime_hint: monetary easing or low real rate period
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL change times FEDFUNDS level) has a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold baseline in simulated trading.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession expansion phase when the unemployment rate is falling from high levels, robust payroll employment growth signals sustainable recovery momentum, which translates into rising corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In the specified regime, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) or the directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in real personal consumption expenditures signals strong aggregate demand, which boosts company sales and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the near term regardless of the broader economic cycle.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS 3-month change against 20-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 4-year rolling window, or the hit ratio is under 52%.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected monthly increase in the CPI index greater than 0.3% month-over-month triggers immediate market repricing of tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces a short-term negative S&P 500 return over the following week.
failure_condition: After CPIAUCSL monthly changes >0.3%, the median 5-day forward return is positive, or the trigger event count is too low (<20) to draw statistical conclusions.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines, the economy is in a 'sweet spot' of rising output and falling slack, which boosts corporate profits and drives strong positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The directional consistency of the joint acceleration-and-decline signal is below 55% over 3-year rolling windows, or the interaction z-score fails to exceed 1.96 in a logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (federal funds rate below 1%), rising inflation is interpreted as a signal of healthy demand growth rather than harmful overheating, leading investors to bid up equities over the following month as real activity expectations improve.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day forward returns is negative or insignificantly positive (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.
